meta.title: MergeCore PyTorch Rules
meta.description: Rules for PyTorch training and inference: device handling, gradients, memory, reproducibility, and data pipelines.
meta.notes: Gate with pytorch: true. Complements mergecore-python-rules.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one training script or module), sum penalties after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Correct gradients, clear device placement, deterministic seeds where needed.
interpretation.7_to_8: Mergeable; tighten eval/train switches or dataloader workers.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: missing zero_grad or no_grad, silent device mismatches.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: training computes wrong loss, leaks memory, or is irreproducible when required.

rules.title: Missing optimizer.zero_grad() in training step
rules.description: Gradients accumulate across calls by default. Forgetting to zero them before backward produces stale, summed gradients and wrong updates.

examples.before: for x, y in loader: out = model(x); loss = criterion(out, y); loss.backward(); optimizer.step()
examples.after: for x, y in loader: optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True); out = model(x); loss = criterion(out, y); loss.backward(); optimizer.step()

rules.title: Evaluation without torch.no_grad() or model.eval()
rules.description: Validation/inference loops should disable grad tracking and switch Dropout/BatchNorm modes. Otherwise you leak gradients and get inconsistent metrics.

rules.title: Hard-coded .cuda() instead of a device variable
rules.description: Hard-coding .cuda() breaks on CPU-only, multi-GPU, and MPS. Use a `device` variable derived once from availability.

rules.title: DataLoader without num_workers or pin_memory on GPU training
rules.description: Single-threaded data loading starves the GPU. Configure num_workers > 0 and pin_memory=True when moving to CUDA.

rules.title: Accumulating tensors that still require grad
rules.description: Collecting `loss` or output tensors into a list without .detach() or .item() retains the entire graph and exhausts memory.

rules.title: Randomness without seed in a benchmarking or evaluation script
rules.description: If the script advertises reproducible metrics, seed torch, numpy, and random; consider torch.use_deterministic_algorithms where relevant.

rules.title: Softmax applied before CrossEntropyLoss
rules.description: nn.CrossEntropyLoss expects raw logits. Applying softmax first double-normalises and degrades training.

rules.title: torch.tensor(...) in the training inner loop
rules.description: Allocating a fresh tensor inside the loop and possibly copying from Python lists is slow. Prefer preallocated buffers or torch.as_tensor where possible.

rules.title: Strong pattern: autocast/GradScaler for mixed precision
rules.description: Using torch.autocast and torch.cuda.amp.GradScaler on CUDA correctly guards fp16 training stability.

rules.title: Tensor dtype or shape assumptions not asserted at boundaries
rules.description: Public forward() methods benefit from explicit shape/dtype checks or type annotations using TensorType to catch wiring errors early.

rules.title: Mixing DataParallel with new code
rules.description: nn.DataParallel is effectively deprecated for new work due to overheads and limitations. Prefer DistributedDataParallel or torch.compile with DDP.

rules.title: Printing loss tensor instead of scalar
rules.description: print(loss) renders the tensor with grad info; calling .item() or formatting with {:.4f} avoids graph retention and produces clean logs.
